element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player must understand team composition, individual player stats, and position substitution mechanics before gameplay
Learning playbook structure and available offensive/defensive formations
Genesis version removes mandatory passing windows by default; players can enable windows via options for guidance
Basic ground game execution alongside passing options
Defensive formation selection and reactive positioning
Individual player stat accumulation across multiple games; persistent progression system

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Variable intensity dependent on score differential and game clock; Genesis version noted for not always generating high-scoring games, creating unpredictable tension cycles compared to SNES counterpart

difficulty_curve.early_game: Adjustable via game options; default difficulty accessible to new players with optional passing window guidance
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increasing opponent AI complexity and play variation as season progresses
difficulty_curve.late_game: Peak opponent difficulty in season playoff/championship scenarios
notable_spikes: Transition from single game to season mode requires sustained performance across multiple games
Playoff games against high-rated NFL teams (1994 roster)

notable_level_patterns.description: One-off contest against selected opponent with real 1994 NFL rosters and player attributes
Extended progression through full 16-game NFL season with persistent player statistics and playoff advancement
Head-to-head competition between human players controlling opposing teams
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Two-player matches (supported across Genesis, SNES, Game Gear versions)
